chunk_summary: This section focuses on counseling about carbohydrate quality, free sugars, and dietary fiber in childhood nutrition, emphasizing WHO and pediatric guidelines for sugar intake, non-sugar sweeteners, and prevention of junk food consumption.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of free sugars and dietary fiber in childhood nutrition.
Learn WHO guidelines on sugar intake and non-sugar sweeteners for children.
Identify strategies to prevent junk food consumption and promote healthy carbohydrate choices.
Explore recommendations for fruit juice consumption in infants and children.
key_facts: WHO recommends limiting free sugar intake to less than 10% of total energy intake for children and adults (2015 guideline).
Non-sugar sweeteners are not recommended for weight control or health benefits in children (WHO, 2023).
Fruit juice consumption should be limited to 4-6 ounces per day for children aged 1-6 years (AAP, 2017).
High dietary fiber intake is associated with improved digestion and reduced risk of non-communicable diseases in children.
Indian Pediatrics guidelines emphasize reducing junk food consumption to prevent childhood obesity and related diseases.
reference_quote: WHO recommends that free sugars should comprise less than 10% of total energy intake for both adults and children to reduce the risk of non-communicable diseases (WHO, 2015).
document_key_topics: Free sugar intake guidelines for children
Role of dietary fiber in childhood nutrition
Strategies to prevent junk food consumption
